title: Allow --userns-uid-map/--userns-gid-map to be global options
body:
Allow --userns-uid-map and --userns-gid-map to be specified as global options, which can be overridden by options specified to commands which know them as non-global options.

This will mainly benefit tools that want to set ID mappings for buildah to use, but which don't want to have to figure out whether or not they're invoking it with a subcommand that recognizes these options.

comments.body: Added checks for `c.GlobalStringSlice()` returning zero-length slices for `--userns-uid-map`/`--userns-gid-map` arguments.

What happens if you specify the options both globally and locally?

podman --uidmap 1000,0,100 build --uidmap 2000,0.100 ~

In podman, nothing unless it adds a similar option.  Buildah's CLI will use the global options to initialize its local store (per `cmd/buildah.getStore()`), which will set the defaults for any images that are newly pulled.  The local options will override those settings in `pkg/parse.IDMappingOptions()`, so `buildah from` and `buildah bud` will use the local options when creating containers.
